compiled_state.title: ticket link task->story child promoted to task->epic making story deps show 0 children
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description: When linking implementation tasks to their parent story via '.claude/scripts/dso ticket link <task-id> <story-id> child', hierarchy enforcement promotes all links to task->epic level (REDIRECT: task->story promoted to task->epic). Consequently, 'ticket deps <story-id>' always shows 0 children even after multiple task creation and linking commands. Tasks are trackable only via name pattern search or by querying the epic deps. This makes story-level progress tracking impossible without workarounds. Observed during sprint execution of epic b885-e9e8 across all 6 stories and 14 tasks.

comments.body: Investigation complete. Root cause confirmed: resolve_hierarchy_link in ticket-graph.py had too-aggressive 'no shared ancestor' logic. When a task (orphan, no parent_id) linked to a story that has a parent epic, the algorithm walked BOTH chains to their roots — task (itself) and epic — resulting in REDIRECT: task→story promoted to task→epic. Fix: in the no-shared-ancestor case, only walk to roots when source_chain depth > 1 (source has ancestors). Orphan sources (depth=1) now keep both IDs as-is. Tests: test_resolve_hierarchy_link_orphan_task_to_story_sc7 + SC7b added to test_ticket_graph.py, confirmed RED before fix and GREEN after.
comments.author: Test
